license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.k9ncz5e
title: ALT/GPT  Detection Assay
author: Zumira Carneiro

steps_raw.instruction: In order to achieve traceable results to IFCC 1 Procedure, is needed the use of the two-reagent method, to occur the enzyme total activation by the pyridoxal phosphate. Preparation the reagent: Add 0.300 mL of the Reagent 3 to a bottle of Reagent 1(24 mL) and mix. Stability: 21 days at 2 – 8 0 C and 24 hours 15 –25 0 C when no chemical or microbial contamination occurs. Optionally, a lower volume of the mixture (Reagent 1 + Reagent 3) may be prepared by using one part of the Reagent 3 to 80 parts of Reference: 'Reference procedure for the Measurement of catalytic Concentration of Alanine Aminotransferase. Cin Chem Lab Med 2002, 40 (70): 718-24.'

Add 0.020 mL of the sample or enzymes calibrator, homogenize and incubate a water- bath at 37 ± 0.2 0 C. Wait five minutes. After this incubation it is possible wait until 30 minutes to start the kinetic determination with the addition of the Reagent 2.
0.040 mL of the Reagent 2, homogenize and transfer immediately to a cuvette at 37 ± 0.2 0 C. Wait one minute.
Measure the initial absorbance (A 1 ). and start simultaneously the timer. Measure the absorbance again after 2 minutes (A 2 ).

It is a usual procedure calculated the enzymatic activity results using a theoretical factor achieved in reaction optimum conditions, described below: Wavelength: 340 nm Cuvette at 37 ± 0.2 0 C, 10 mm light path. Pass band ≤2 nm Sray light ≤ 0.1% If one of the correlated parameters is modified, it is recommended to apply an enzymes calibrator indicated by the reagent manufacturer. Labtest Diagnostica recommends Calibra series to perform the ALT/GPT system calibration. DA/minute(test or calibrator) = (A1-A2)/2 Factor: (Calibrator activity)/(DA/minute (test) x factor ALT activity (U/L) = DA/minute (test) x Factor If all the correlated parameters are fulfilled, the theoretical factor (1746) can be applied.
In a test tube labeled “test” or “Calibrator”, Add 0.160 mL of the mixture Reagent 1 + Reagent 3.
Perform a water Blank measurement at 340 nm.
